text: This is the story of C. V. Raju, a grassroots innovator from Andhra Pradesh. A traditional wooden and lacquer toy-making craft in his region was declining as artisans struggled to sustain it commercially. He worked to revive and promote the traditional toy-making craft, helping it gain recognition (including a UNESCO Seal of Excellence for Handicrafts) and reach new markets.

label.problem: A traditional wooden and lacquer toy-making craft in his region was declining as artisans struggled to sustain it commercially.
label.solution: He worked to revive and promote the traditional toy-making craft, helping it gain recognition (including a UNESCO Seal of Excellence for Handicrafts) and reach new markets.

text: In Andhra Pradesh, a traditional wooden and lacquer toy-making craft in his region was declining as artisans struggled to sustain it commercially. C. V. Raju responded with a practical fix: he worked to revive and promote the traditional toy-making craft, helping it gain recognition (including a UNESCO Seal of Excellence for Handicrafts) and reach new markets.

label.problem: A traditional wooden and lacquer toy-making craft in his region was declining as artisans struggled to sustain it commercially.
label.solution: He worked to revive and promote the traditional toy-making craft, helping it gain recognition (including a UNESCO Seal of Excellence for Handicrafts) and reach new markets.

text: In Idukki, Kerala, threshing pepper berries from their stalks by hand was slow and tedious for growers. Binoy Sebastian responded with a practical fix: he built a rotating-drum pepper thresher with spiral blades that rubs berries free of their stalks at high efficiency, validated by the local Krishi Vigyan Kendra.

label.problem: Threshing pepper berries from their stalks by hand was slow and tedious for growers.
label.solution: He built a rotating-drum pepper thresher with spiral blades that rubs berries free of their stalks at high efficiency, validated by the local Krishi Vigyan Kendra.

text: In Sivasagar, Assam, traditional Muga silk reeling was slow and labour-intensive, and commercial reeling machines were too costly for him to afford. Saruj Chetia responded with a practical fix: he built a low-cost reeling machine, refined from an initial sewing-machine-parts prototype, that reels both warp and weft silk yarn simultaneously.

label.problem: Traditional Muga silk reeling was slow and labour-intensive, and commercial reeling machines were too costly for him to afford.
label.solution: He built a low-cost reeling machine, refined from an initial sewing-machine-parts prototype, that reels both warp and weft silk yarn simultaneously.

text: A traditional wooden and lacquer toy-making craft in his region was declining as artisans struggled to sustain it commercially. That was the situation C. V. Raju set out to change in Andhra Pradesh. He worked to revive and promote the traditional toy-making craft, helping it gain recognition (including a UNESCO Seal of Excellence for Handicrafts) and reach new markets.

label.problem: A traditional wooden and lacquer toy-making craft in his region was declining as artisans struggled to sustain it commercially.
label.solution: He worked to revive and promote the traditional toy-making craft, helping it gain recognition (including a UNESCO Seal of Excellence for Handicrafts) and reach new markets.

text: Mousumi Biswas, from Murshidabad, West Bengal, noticed that farmers in her area lacked an aromatic rice variety that combined high yield with tolerance to blast disease. To solve this, by crossing and selecting parent rice varieties over several years, she developed M-Jamini, a fine-grained, aromatic rice variety that yields well and resists blast disease.

label.problem: Farmers in her area lacked an aromatic rice variety that combined high yield with tolerance to blast disease.
label.solution: By crossing and selecting parent rice varieties over several years, she developed M-Jamini, a fine-grained, aromatic rice variety that yields well and resists blast disease.

text: This is the story of Appachan, a grassroots innovator from Kerala. Workers who climb tall coconut and palm trees to harvest nuts or maintain electric poles faced a slow, physically risky climb with no safety support. He built a simple mechanical tree-climbing device that lets a person ascend tall trunks safely and quickly, reducing the risk of falls during harvesting or pole maintenance.

label.problem: Workers who climb tall coconut and palm trees to harvest nuts or maintain electric poles faced a slow, physically risky climb with no safety support.
label.solution: He built a simple mechanical tree-climbing device that lets a person ascend tall trunks safely and quickly, reducing the risk of falls during harvesting or pole maintenance.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — scraping lac resin from tree branches by hand with a knife or by beating sticks was slow, limited a worker to just a few kilograms a day, and left the scraped lac full of dirt and impurities — Sourav Dey came up with a grassroots solution. Inspired by a sugarcane juice extractor, he designed a manually operated roller-type lac scraper that removes lac from branches without breaking them, leaving it cleaner and easier to process.

label.problem: Scraping lac resin from tree branches by hand with a knife or by beating sticks was slow, limited a worker to just a few kilograms a day, and left the scraped lac full of dirt and impurities.
label.solution: Inspired by a sugarcane juice extractor, he designed a manually operated roller-type lac scraper that removes lac from branches without breaking them, leaving it cleaner and easier to process.

text: In Raigarh, Chhattisgarh, farmers growing sunflower at scale in his village struggled to extract seeds without expensive machinery, cutting into their returns. Lakhanlal Patel responded with a practical fix: he built a low-cost, motor-powered sunflower seed extractor using rubber-belt beaters that deseeds sunflowers with over 95 percent efficiency.

label.problem: Farmers growing sunflower at scale in his village struggled to extract seeds without expensive machinery, cutting into their returns.
label.solution: He built a low-cost, motor-powered sunflower seed extractor using rubber-belt beaters that deseeds sunflowers with over 95 percent efficiency.

text: Saruj Chetia, from Sivasagar, Assam, noticed that traditional Muga silk reeling was slow and labour-intensive, and commercial reeling machines were too costly for him to afford. To solve this, he built a low-cost reeling machine, refined from an initial sewing-machine-parts prototype, that reels both warp and weft silk yarn simultaneously.

label.problem: Traditional Muga silk reeling was slow and labour-intensive, and commercial reeling machines were too costly for him to afford.
label.solution: He built a low-cost reeling machine, refined from an initial sewing-machine-parts prototype, that reels both warp and weft silk yarn simultaneously.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — farmers growing sunflower at scale in his village struggled to extract seeds without expensive machinery, cutting into their returns — Lakhanlal Patel came up with a grassroots solution. He built a low-cost, motor-powered sunflower seed extractor using rubber-belt beaters that deseeds sunflowers with over 95 percent efficiency.

label.problem: Farmers growing sunflower at scale in his village struggled to extract seeds without expensive machinery, cutting into their returns.
label.solution: He built a low-cost, motor-powered sunflower seed extractor using rubber-belt beaters that deseeds sunflowers with over 95 percent efficiency.

text: Cultivating paddy in the hilly terrain of his district was difficult, and transporting rice from other areas added time and cost. That was the situation Aji Thomas set out to change in Wayanad, Kerala. He developed a table-top pelletisation system that grows paddy saplings in nutrient-rich pellets, increasing tiller count and straw yield while cutting seed requirements sharply.

label.problem: Cultivating paddy in the hilly terrain of his district was difficult, and transporting rice from other areas added time and cost.
label.solution: He developed a table-top pelletisation system that grows paddy saplings in nutrient-rich pellets, increasing tiller count and straw yield while cutting seed requirements sharply.

text: This is the story of Vishnu Kumar Sharma, a grassroots innovator from Jaipur, Rajasthan. Dairy farmers had no simple treatment for mastitis, an udder infection that causes pain, swelling, and reduced milk quality in cows. He developed a herbal formulation that, applied to the affected udder, gradually relieves the symptoms of mastitis.

label.problem: Dairy farmers had no simple treatment for mastitis, an udder infection that causes pain, swelling, and reduced milk quality in cows.
label.solution: He developed a herbal formulation that, applied to the affected udder, gradually relieves the symptoms of mastitis.

label.solution: He developed a table-top pelletisation system that grows paddy saplings in nutrient-rich pellets, increasing tiller count and straw yield while cutting seed requirements sharply.

label.problem: Scraping lac resin from tree branches by hand with a knife or by beating sticks was slow, limited a worker to just a few kilograms a day, and left the scraped lac full of dirt and impurities.
label.solution: Inspired by a sugarcane juice extractor, he designed a manually operated roller-type lac scraper that removes lac from branches without breaking them, leaving it cleaner and easier to process.

text: In Pune, Maharashtra, grape growers wanted a black grape variety with bigger bunches, higher sugar content, and better shelf life than the standard Sharad Seedless. Vithal Nivruti Thorat responded with a practical fix: by selecting and grafting distinct vines he spotted in his own orchard, he developed the Nath Jumbo Seedless grape, prized for its bold berries, sweetness, and export-quality shelf life.

label.problem: Grape growers wanted a black grape variety with bigger bunches, higher sugar content, and better shelf life than the standard Sharad Seedless.
label.solution: By selecting and grafting distinct vines he spotted in his own orchard, he developed the Nath Jumbo Seedless grape, prized for its bold berries, sweetness, and export-quality shelf life.

text: Cup hangers used to collect rubber latex during tapping were made by hand, so no two were alike and some failed under the weight of collected latex. That was the situation Mohana Kumar G S set out to change in Kollam, Kerala. He built a motorised machine that automatically produces uniform, latex-resistant cup hangers at a rate of 1,200 an hour.

label.problem: Cup hangers used to collect rubber latex during tapping were made by hand, so no two were alike and some failed under the weight of collected latex.
label.solution: He built a motorised machine that automatically produces uniform, latex-resistant cup hangers at a rate of 1,200 an hour.

He built a simple mechanical tree-climbing device that lets a person ascend tall trunks safely and quickly, reducing the risk of falls during harvesting or pole maintenance.

text: Selichum Sangtam, from Tuensang, Nagaland, noticed that farmers plucking and pruning cardamom with ordinary knives and sickles often injured the plants and capsules in the process. To solve this, he designed curved plucking and dual-edged pruning tools that harvest cardamom capsules and clean the plants without damaging them.

label.problem: Farmers plucking and pruning cardamom with ordinary knives and sickles often injured the plants and capsules in the process.
label.solution: He designed curved plucking and dual-edged pruning tools that harvest cardamom capsules and clean the plants without damaging them.

text: Faced with a familiar rural problem — small farmers spraying pesticide by hand found it slow, tiring, and imprecise, especially over larger fields — Mansukhbhai Jagani came up with a grassroots solution. He built a bicycle-mounted pesticide sprayer where pedalling powers the spray pump, letting one person cover a field faster and with less physical strain than manual spraying.

label.solution: He built a bicycle-mounted pesticide sprayer where pedalling powers the spray pump, letting one person cover a field faster and with less physical strain than manual spraying.

label.problem: Farmers in her area lacked an aromatic rice variety that combined high yield with tolerance to blast disease.
label.solution: By crossing and selecting parent rice varieties over several years, she developed M-Jamini, a fine-grained, aromatic rice variety that yields well and resists blast disease.

text: Small farmers spraying pesticide by hand found it slow, tiring, and imprecise, especially over larger fields. That was the situation Mansukhbhai Jagani set out to change in Gujarat. He built a bicycle-mounted pesticide sprayer where pedalling powers the spray pump, letting one person cover a field faster and with less physical strain than manual spraying.
